heading: Collection and disbursement of support payments
label: 654b Collection and disbursement of support payments
notes: References in TextFor effective date of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(4), see Effective Date note below.

Amendments1997—Subsec. (c)(1). inserted at end “The date of collection for amounts collected and distributed under this part is the date of receipt by the State disbursement unit, except that if current support is withheld by an employer in the month when due and is received by the State disbursement unit in a month other than the month when due, the date of withholding may be deemed to be the date of collection.”Pub. children.label: A
children.text: in all cases being enforced by the State pursuant to ; andsection 654(4) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in all cases not being enforced by the State under this part in which the support order is initially issued in the State on or after , and in which the income of the noncustodial parent is subject to withholding pursuant to .January 1, 1994section 666(a)(8)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: directly by the State agency (or 2 or more State agencies under a regional cooperative agreement), or (to the extent appropriate) by a contractor responsible directly to the State agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: except in cases described in paragraph (1)(B), in coordination with the automated system established by the State pursuant to .section 654a of this title

children.heading: Linking of local disbursement units
children.label: 3 Linking of local disbursement units
children.text: The State disbursement unit may be established by linking local disbursement units through an automated information network, subject to this section, if the Secretary agrees that the system will not cost more nor take more time to establish or operate than a centralized system. In addition, employers shall be given 1 location to which income withholding is sent.
for receipt of payments from parents, employers, and other States, and for disbursements to custodial parents and other obligees, the State agency, and the agencies of other States;
to ensure prompt disbursement of the custodial parent’s share of any payment; and

to furnish to any parent, upon request, timely information on the current status of support payments under an order requiring payments to be made by or to the parent, except that in cases described in subsection (a)(1)(B), the State disbursement unit shall not be required to convert and maintain in automated form records of payments kept pursuant to before the effective date of this section.section 666(a)(8)(B)(iii) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the State disbursement unit shall distribute all amounts payable under within 2 business days after receipt from the employer or other source of periodic income, if sufficient information identifying the payee is provided. The date of collection for amounts collected and distributed under this part is the date of receipt by the State disbursement unit, except that if current support is withheld by an employer in the month when due and is received by the State disbursement unit in a month other than the month when due, the date of withholding may be deemed to be the date of collection.section 657(a) of this title

children.heading: Permissive retention of arrearages
children.label: 2 Permissive retention of arrearages
children.text: The State disbursement unit may delay the distribution of collections toward arrearages until the resolution of any timely appeal with respect to such arrearages.

children.heading: “Business day” defined
children.label: d “Business day” defined
children.text: As used in this section, the term “business day” means a day on which State offices are open for regular business.
